summary: Freshly synthesized glucose-6-phosphate from glycogenesis stays undegraded because it occurs in the cytoplasm while glucose-6-phosphatase acts exclusively within the endoplasmic reticulum. This ensures proper regulation between glycogen storage and release processes without immediate degradation.
